text.title: Reporting Dashboards Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying reporting-dashboards. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Leadership receives "0% completion" digest; sprint actually shipped most items; trust in report collapses.
antipattern.root-cause: Query authentication or JQL silently failed; fetch returned empty; metric computed correctly on empty input; validation gate missing.
antipattern.fix: Add validate-before-send rule: committed>0 + fetch_errors==0 required. On fail emit error alert + exit non-zero. Never ship the 0% post.

antipattern.symptom: Sprint marked "On Track" because completion_rate=92%; in reality 10 issues added on day 4 + 4 blocked = team thrashing.
antipattern.root-cause: Health derived from completion alone; scope_creep + blocked_count not in formula.
antipattern.fix: Use multi-metric health: health = f(completion_rate, scope_creep, blocked_count). Document threshold table in schema.

antipattern.symptom: Stakeholder asks "what was last week's velocity?"; the Slack message is deleted; no historical record.
antipattern.root-cause: Pipeline collapsed generate + deliver; rendered output never written to durable store.
antipattern.fix: Archive rendered Markdown + raw JSON to S3/Notion/repo before delivery; delivery message links to archive.

antipattern.symptom: Bug in fetch script accidentally mutates issue status; recovery costs hours.
antipattern.root-cause: PM-tool token granted write scopes when read-only is sufficient.
antipattern.fix: Provision a separate read-only token for the reporting pipeline. Rotate writers and readers independently.
